name: CROWN INNOVATION
address: 347 SE LOGSDEN STREET STE 100, BEND, OR 97702

fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency response operations to this property could be achieved by considering modifications to the access and circulation layout. Introducing additional access points may facilitate quicker entry and exit for emergency vehicles, potentially improving response times. Ensuring that the current pedestrian walkways are wide and unobstructed could support efficient movement of personnel and equipment during an emergency. Improving the design and maintenance of landscaped areas to prevent obstruction of sightlines may enhance visibility, aiding responders in locating structures and key areas rapidly. Modifications or clearly marked gates in the perimeter fencing, especially at the rear, could provide alternative access points, supporting flexible responder navigation and reducing reliance on a single entry point. Reviewing the layout of the vehicle parking area to ensure that emergency vehicles have sufficient room to maneuver and stage might further augment response capabilities.

wildfire.recommendations: Improving the separation between vegetation and the structure could enhance defensible space and decrease potential exposure to wildfire. Pruning lower branches of scattered trees and creating space between them may help reduce fuel continuity and prevent the spread of fire across open terrain. Expanding open areas adjacent to the structure through selective removal and thinning of vegetation can further mitigate exposure. Prioritizing the management of vegetation in close proximity to the building, including grass and shrubs, could create a more defensible space. Additionally, regularly maintaining these areas to prevent the accumulation of leaves and other combustible material may further support property preparedness. Continuously monitoring and adapting vegetation management practices will also support long-term resilience against wildfire impacts.
